scenarios.blocker_reason: Plan adds cardio (Zone 2 bike sessions Wed in W1-W3) despite athlete explicitly stating does_cardio=false, and only schedules 5 lifting days/week instead of the required 6.

verdict.blocker_reason: Plan adds cardio (Zone 2 bike sessions Wed in W1-W3) despite athlete explicitly stating does_cardio=false, and only schedules 5 lifting days/week instead of the required 6.

constraint_adherence.reasoning: Hard violations: (1) Athlete specified lifting_days=6 and does_cardio=false, but plan delivers only 5 lifting days (Mon/Tue/Thu/Fri/Sat) with Wed as a bike session in W1-W3 — this both undershoots lift count AND adds disallowed cardio. (2) W4 deload further drops to only 4 lifting days. The narrative even admits 'Split: Upper/Lower (4 lifting days) + 2 accessory/hypertrophy days' which contradicts the 6 lifting days requested.

periodization.reasoning: 3+1 structure is appropriate: W1 baseline RPE7, W2 +1 rep, W3 +1 set at RPE8, W4 deload with ~37% volume cut and intensity preserved (same loads, fewer sets) — textbook accumulation. Progression uses one mechanism at a time as principles dictate. Minor quibble: W4 Wed/Sat/Sun all rest is excessive; could maintain a light session.

intensity_distribution.reasoning: Heavy lower days (Mon Squat, Thu Deadlift) are spaced 72h apart with upper days between — good sequencing. RPE 7-8 on main lifts and 6-7 on accessories matches accumulation targets. No consecutive lower days. No RPE creep into 9-10 territory.

specificity.reasoning: Every session lists specific exercises with sets×reps×RPE (e.g., W1 Mon Back Squat 4×6 RPE7, W3 Thu Deadlift 5×5 RPE8). Each block has a one-line rationale. Warmup/cooldown durations specified. Progression notes ('+1 rep vs W1') are concrete and actionable.

scenario_fit.reasoning: Capped at 2 then lowered further due to two must_not_have violations: (1) Cardio sessions added on Wed W1-W3 despite athlete saying no cardio — explicit must_not_have violation. (2) Must-have of 6 lifting days unmet (only 5, dropping to 4 in deload). Each muscle is hit 2x/week and set volumes are reasonable (12-18/muscle), but the cardio inclusion and day-count miss are direct contract breaches.

strengths: Clean 3:1 accumulation periodization with W3 peak (5×6 RPE8 on compounds) and a genuine W4 deload that cuts volume ~37% while preserving load (e.g., Squat 5×6→3×5 same weight)
Excellent session specificity — every lift has sets×reps×RPE and progression notes (e.g., W2 Bench '4×7 +1 rep vs W1', W3 '+1 set, +2.5kg if W1 was RPE6')
Smart heavy-lower spacing: Squat Mon and Deadlift Thu give 72h between heavy hip/knee dominant work
weaknesses: Plan delivers 5 lifting days (W1-W3) and only 4 in W4, not the requested 6 lifting_days — direct constraint violation
Wed Zone 2 bike sessions in W1-W3 directly violate the athlete's stated 'no cardio' preference and the scenario's must_not_have

Narrative contradicts itself — header says 'upper_lower' and 'no cardio' goal, then plan body inserts bike sessions and explains them as 'minimal cardio to limit interference,' which the athlete never asked for

scenarios.plan_summary:
Split: upper_lower
Narrative: 4-week hypertrophy-focused strength block for a 27-year-old intermediate male. Goal is muscle gain with strength emphasis. The athlete is already lifting 5x/week with 65-min sessions and has solid recovery markers (HRV 58, sleep 7.2h). No running background, so cardio is kept minimal (2x short Zone 2 bike/walk sessions per week) to limit interference with hypertrophy adaptations.

**Split:** Upper/Lower (4 lifting days) + 2 accessory/hypertrophy days + 1 full rest day. This hits each muscle group 2x/week at 14-18 working sets per group — squarely in the intermediate hypertrophy sweet spot.

**Periodization:** Accumulation phase (Weeks 1-3, RPE 6-8) → Deload (Week 4, volume −37%, intensity preserved). Main compounds are Squat, Romanian Deadlift, Bench Press, Barbell Row. Accessory work follows. Progressive overload: Weeks 1→2 add a rep, Week 2→3 add a set or load, Week 4 deloads.

**Intensity distribution:** Quality (heavy compound) sessions never on consecutive days. Lower-body heavy sessions are spaced 48h+ apart. Short Zone 2 cardio sessions are placed on upper-body days and kept ≤25 min to minimize interference.

**Phase:** Accumulation — RPE 6-8, 3-4 working sets, emphasis on volume and technique. Progressive overload via reps, then sets, then load.
Phase: base
Deload every: 3 weeks
Risks called out by planner: Transitioning from 5 lifting days to a structured 4-day compound split may feel like reduced volume initially — trust the process; quality sets near RPE 8 outperform junk volume | No recent cardio base means Zone 2 sessions should stay very easy (RPE 4-5) to avoid excessive fatigue bleed into lifting sessions | Watch for RPE creep on Squat and Bench between sets — if Set 2 jumps >2 RPE units above Set 1, reduce load rather than grinding through

── Week 1: Establish rhythm — calibrate effort levels, build volume base at RPE 6-7 ──
  Mon
    AM: [strength] Lower A — Squat Focus — Start the week fresh with the most demanding lower-body compound movement. 
      • WARMUP General warm-up 10min
      • WORK Primary compound — establish baseline Back Squat 4×6 RPE7
      • WORK Posterior chain accessory Romanian Deadlift 3×10 RPE7
      • WORK Quad volume Leg Press 3×12 RPE7
      • WORK Hamstring isolation Leg Curl (Machine) 3×12 RPE7
      • WORK Calf volume Calf Raise (Standing) 3×15 RPE7
      • COOLDOWN Lower body stretch 5min
  Tue
    AM: [strength] Upper A — Horizontal Push/Pull — Upper body day follows Lower A, protecting squat pattern from fatigue while accumulating horizontal pushing and pulling volume. 
      • WARMUP Band pull-aparts + shoulder CARs 10min
      • WORK Primary horizontal push Barbell Bench Press 4×6 RPE7
      • WORK Primary horizontal pull Barbell Bent-Over Row 4×6 RPE7
      • WORK Upper chest volume Incline Dumbbell Press 3×10 RPE7
      • WORK Mid-back volume Seated Cable Row 3×12 RPE7
      • WORK Delt isolation Dumbbell Lateral Raise 3×15 RPE7
      • WORK Bicep volume EZ-Bar Curl 3×12 RPE7
      • WORK Tricep volume Tricep Pushdown (Cable) 3×12 RPE7
      • COOLDOWN Pec + lat stretch 5min
  Wed
    AM: [bike] Zone 2 Easy Bike — 20 min — Low-intensity aerobic session maintains cardiovascular health with minimal interference to strength adaptations. 
      • WARMUP Easy spin 5min Z1 45% FTP
      • WORK Steady Zone 2 — conversational, nasal breathe 20min Z2 55% FTP
      • COOLDOWN Easy spin down 5min Z1 40% FTP
  Thu
    AM: [strength] Lower B — Deadlift Focus — Hip-hinge dominant session 72h after Squat session — sufficient recovery for quality heavy pulling. 
      • WARMUP Hip mobility + banded walks 10min
      • WORK Primary hip hinge — bracing focus Conventional Deadlift 4×5 RPE7
      • WORK Unilateral quad/glute volume Bulgarian Split Squat 3×10 RPE7
      • WORK Hamstring eccentric loading Glute-Ham Raise / Nordic Curl 3×8 RPE7
      • WORK Glute max isolation Hip Thrust (Barbell) 3×12 RPE7
      • WORK Lower leg prehab Tibialis Raise 2×20 RPE6
      • COOLDOWN Hip flexor + hamstring stretch 5min
  Fri
    AM: [strength] Upper B — Vertical Push/Pull — Vertical plane session complements Tuesday's horizontal work, ensuring full upper-body muscle group coverage twice weekly. 
      • WARMUP Shoulder warm-up + face pulls 10min
      • WORK Primary vertical push Overhead Press (Barbell) 4×6 RPE7
      • WORK Primary vertical pull Pull-Up / Lat Pulldown 4×8 RPE7
      • WORK Delt volume Arnold Press (Dumbbell) 3×10 RPE7
      • WORK Lat + rear delt volume Single-Arm Dumbbell Row 3×12 RPE7
      • WORK Rear delt / external rotator health Face Pull (Cable) 3×15 RPE6
      • WORK Brachialis / bicep volume Hammer Curl 3×12 RPE7
      • WORK Long head tricep volume Overhead Tricep Extension (Cable) 3×12 RPE7
      • COOLDOWN Shoulder + lat stretch 5min
  Sat
    AM: [strength] Full-Body Accessory — Weak Point Work — Saturday accessory session fills in weak points and adds volume where needed without heavy CNS demand, keeping Sunday free for full rest. 
      • WARMUP General movement prep 10min
      • WORK Quad / mobility reinforcement Goblet Squat 3×15 RPE6
      • WORK Chest isolation / stretch Cable Fly 3×15 RPE6
      • WORK Mid-back volume Chest-Supported Row (Machine) 3×15 RPE6
      • WORK Lateral delt isolation Lateral Raise (Cable) 3×15 RPE6
      • WORK Bicep long head stretch Incline Dumbbell Curl 3×12 RPE6
      • WORK Tricep volume Skull Crusher (EZ-Bar) 3×12 RPE6
      • WORK Core anti-extension Ab Wheel Rollout 3×10 RPE7
      • COOLDOWN Full body stretch 5min
  Sun (REST)
    (full rest)

── Week 2: Progress volume — add 1 rep to compounds, RPE nudged to 7-8, same structure ──
  Mon
    AM: [strength] Lower A — Squat Focus (W2) — Adding one rep per set on the squat is the simplest overload mechanism — do not add load unless RPE was below 6 in Week 1. 
      • WARMUP General warm-up 10min
      • WORK Add 1 rep vs Week 1 — same or +2.5kg Back Squat 4×7 RPE7
      • WORK +1 rep vs W1 Romanian Deadlift 3×11 RPE7
      • WORK +1 rep vs W1 Leg Press 3×13 RPE7
      • WORK +1 rep vs W1 Leg Curl (Machine) 3×13 RPE7
      • WORK +1 rep vs W1 Calf Raise (Standing) 3×16 RPE7
      • COOLDOWN Lower body stretch 5min
  Tue
    AM: [strength] Upper A — Horizontal Push/Pull (W2) — Progressive overload via +1 rep on compounds; accessories also progress 1 rep for consistent stimulus. 
      • WARMUP Band pull-aparts + shoulder CARs 10min
      • WORK +1 rep vs W1 Barbell Bench Press 4×7 RPE7
      • WORK +1 rep vs W1 Barbell Bent-Over Row 4×7 RPE7
      • WORK +1 rep vs W1 Incline Dumbbell Press 3×11 RPE7
      • WORK +1 rep vs W1 Seated Cable Row 3×13 RPE7
      • WORK +1 rep vs W1 Dumbbell Lateral Raise 3×16 RPE7
      • WORK +1 rep vs W1 EZ-Bar Curl 3×13 RPE7
      • WORK +1 rep vs W1 Tricep Pushdown (Cable) 3×13 RPE7
      • COOLDOWN Pec + lat stretch 5min
  Wed
    AM: [bike] Zone 2 Easy Bike — 20 min — Identical to Week 1 — cardio volume stays fixed to avoid creeping interference with the strength overload happening this week. 
      • WARMUP Easy spin 5min Z1 45% FTP
      • WORK Steady Zone 2 — conversational, nasal breathe 20min Z2 55% FTP
      • COOLDOWN Easy spin down 5min Z1 40% FTP
  Thu
    AM: [strength] Lower B — Deadlift Focus (W2) — Adding one rep to deadlift and accessories keeps overload systematic without introducing new loading risk. 
      • WARMUP Hip mobility + banded walks 10min
      • WORK +1 rep vs W1 Conventional Deadlift 4×6 RPE7
      • WORK +1 rep vs W1 Bulgarian Split Squat 3×11 RPE7
      • WORK +1 rep vs W1 Glute-Ham Raise / Nordic Curl 3×9 RPE7
      • WORK +1 rep vs W1 Hip Thrust (Barbell) 3×13 RPE7
      • WORK Maintained — prehab only Tibialis Raise 2×20 RPE6
      • COOLDOWN Hip flexor + hamstring stretch 5min
  Fri
    AM: [strength] Upper B — Vertical Push/Pull (W2) — Vertical pull and push continue to progress by 1 rep — shoulder and lat volume builds toward Week 3 set addition. 
      • WARMUP Shoulder warm-up + face pulls 10min
      • WORK +1 rep vs W1 Overhead Press (Barbell) 4×7 RPE7
      • WORK +1 rep vs W1 Pull-Up / Lat Pulldown 4×9 RPE7
      • WORK +1 rep vs W1 Arnold Press (Dumbbell) 3×11 RPE7
      • WORK +1 rep vs W1 Single-Arm Dumbbell Row 3×13 RPE7
      • WORK Maintained — shoulder health Face Pull (Cable) 3×15 RPE6
      • WORK +1 rep vs W1 Hammer Curl 3×13 RPE7
      • WORK +1 rep vs W1 Overhead Tricep Extension (Cable) 3×13 RPE7
      • COOLDOWN Shoulder + lat stretch 5min
  Sat
    AM: [strength] Full-Body Accessory — Weak Point Work (W2) — Accessory volume progresses identically — 1 extra rep per exercise to maintain consistent overload signal. 
      • WARMUP General movement prep 10min
      • WORK +1 rep vs W1 Goblet Squat 3×16 RPE6
      • WORK +1 rep vs W1 Cable Fly 3×16 RPE6
      • WORK +1 rep vs W1 Chest-Supported Row (Machine) 3×16 RPE6
      • WORK +1 rep vs W1 Lateral Raise (Cable) 3×16 RPE6
      • WORK +1 rep vs W1 Incline Dumbbell Curl 3×13 RPE6
      • WORK +1 rep vs W1 Skull Crusher (EZ-Bar) 3×13 RPE6
      • WORK +1 rep vs W1 Ab Wheel Rollout 3×11 RPE7
      • COOLDOWN Full body stretch 5min
  Sun (REST)
    (full rest)

── Week 3: Peak loading — add 1 working set to all compounds, RPE 8, highest volume of the block ──
  Mon
    AM: [strength] Lower A — Squat Focus (W3, Peak Load) — Adding a 5th working set is the primary overload mechanism this week — combined with a load increase from W1, this represents the highest stress point of the block. 
      • WARMUP General warm-up 10min
      • WORK +1 set vs W2, +2.5-5kg vs W1 baseline Back Squat 5×6 RPE8
      • WORK +1 set vs W2 Romanian Deadlift 4×10 RPE8
      • WORK +1 set vs W2 Leg Press 4×12 RPE8
      • WORK Maintained sets, up weight slightly Leg Curl (Machine) 3×12 RPE8
      • WORK Maintained — calf endurance focus Calf Raise (Standing) 3×15 RPE7
      • COOLDOWN Lower body stretch 5min
  Tue
    AM: [strength] Upper A — Horizontal Push/Pull (W3, Peak Load) — Peak volume week — adding a 5th set on primary compounds while maintaining RPE 8 drives maximum hypertrophic stimulus before the deload. 
      • WARMUP Band pull-aparts + shoulder CARs 10min
      • WORK +1 set vs W2, +2.5kg vs W1 if W1 was RPE 6 Barbell Bench Press 5×6 RPE8
      • WORK +1 set vs W2 Barbell Bent-Over Row 5×6 RPE8
      • WORK Maintained sets, RPE up Incline Dumbbell Press 3×10 RPE8
      • WORK Maintained sets, RPE up Seated Cable Row 3×12 RPE8
      • WORK Maintained Dumbbell Lateral Raise 3×15 RPE7
      • WORK RPE up from W2 EZ-Bar Curl 3×12 RPE8
      • WORK RPE up from W2 Tricep Pushdown (Cable) 3×12 RPE8
      • COOLDOWN Pec + lat stretch 5min
  Wed
    AM: [bike] Zone 2 Easy Bike — 20 min — Peak loading week means the easy day must truly be easy — keep Zone 2 cardio unchanged to ensure full recovery for Thursday's heavy lower session. 
      • WARMUP Easy spin 5min Z1 45% FTP
      • WORK Steady Zone 2 — nasal breathe, no pushing 20min Z2 55% FTP
      • COOLDOWN Easy spin down 5min Z1 40% FTP
  Thu
    AM: [strength] Lower B — Deadlift Focus (W3, Peak Load) — Highest deadlift volume of the block — 5 sets at RPE 8 with load progressed from W1. This is the most demanding session; eat and sleep well the night before. 
      • WARMUP Hip mobility + banded walks 10min
      • WORK +1 set, +2.5-5kg vs W1 Conventional Deadlift 5×5 RPE8
      • WORK +1 set vs W2 Bulgarian Split Squat 4×10 RPE8
      • WORK RPE up vs W2 Glute-Ham Raise / Nordic Curl 3×8 RPE8
      • WORK RPE up, add load Hip Thrust (Barbell) 3×12 RPE8
      • WORK Maintained — prehab Tibialis Raise 2×20 RPE6
      • COOLDOWN Hip flexor + hamstring stretch 5min
  Fri
    AM: [strength] Upper B — Vertical Push/Pull (W3, Peak Load) — 5th set added to primary vertical movements, completing the peak loading week for the upper body. 
      • WARMUP Shoulder warm-up + face pulls 10min
      • WORK +1 set, RPE 8 — use same load as W2 to manage fatigue Overhead Press (Barbell) 5×6 RPE8
      • WORK +1 set vs W2 Pull-Up / Lat Pulldown 5×8 RPE8
      • WORK RPE up vs W2 Arnold Press (Dumbbell) 3×10 RPE8
      • WORK RPE up vs W2 Single-Arm Dumbbell Row 3×12 RPE8
      • WORK Maintained — rotator cuff health Face Pull (Cable) 3×15 RPE6
      • WORK RPE up vs W2 Hammer Curl 3×12 RPE8
      • WORK RPE up vs W2 Overhead Tricep Extension (Cable) 3×12 RPE8
      • COOLDOWN Shoulder + lat stretch 5min
  Sat
    AM: [strength] Full-Body Accessory — Weak Point Work (W3) — Accessory volume kept moderate on Saturday to ensure the body can absorb the heavy compound work from Mon-Fri without excessive systemic fatigue. 
      • WARMUP General movement prep 10min
      • WORK Moderate — mobility focus Goblet Squat 3×15 RPE7
      • WORK Chest stretch emphasis Cable Fly 3×15 RPE7
      • WORK Mid-back recovery volume Chest-Supported Row (Machine) 3×15 RPE7
      • WORK Delt isolation Lateral Raise (Cable) 3×15 RPE7
      • WORK Bicep stretch Incline Dumbbell Curl 3×12 RPE7
      • WORK Tricep long head Skull Crusher (EZ-Bar) 3×12 RPE7
      • WORK Core — same as W1 Ab Wheel Rollout 3×10 RPE7
      • COOLDOWN Full body stretch 5min
  Sun (REST)
    (full rest)

── Week 4: Deload — volume cut ~37%, intensity preserved; 2 short quality sessions, flush fatigue ──
  Mon
    AM: [strength] Deload Lower A — Squat (Reduced Volume) — Deload cuts sets by ~40% and reps per set; loads stay the same to maintain neuromuscular readiness. This is not a vacation — it is structured recovery. 
      • WARMUP General warm-up 10min
      • WORK Deload — same load, 3 sets only, stop well short of failure Back Squat 3×5 RPE6
      • WORK Deload — reduced sets Romanian Deadlift 2×8 RPE6
      • WORK Deload — reduced sets Leg Press 2×10 RPE6
      • WORK Deload — reduced sets Leg Curl (Machine) 2×10 RPE6
      • COOLDOWN Lower body stretch 5min
  Tue
    AM: [strength] Deload Upper A — Horizontal Push/Pull — Upper body deload mirrors lower: same loads, fewer sets, clear intent to flush fatigue rather than accumulate more. 
      • WARMUP Band pull-aparts + shoulder CARs 10min
      • WORK Deload — same load, 3 sets, RPE 6 Barbell Bench Press 3×5 RPE6
      • WORK Deload — reduced volume Barbell Bent-Over Row 3×5 RPE6
      • WORK Deload accessory Incline Dumbbell Press 2×8 RPE6
      • WORK Deload accessory Seated Cable Row 2×10 RPE6
      • WORK Deload accessory EZ-Bar Curl 2×10 RPE6
      • WORK Deload accessory Tricep Pushdown (Cable) 2×10 RPE6
      • COOLDOWN Pec + lat stretch 5min
  Wed (REST)
    (full rest)
  Thu
    AM: [strength] Deload Lower B — Deadlift (Reduced Volume) — Deload deadlift session — same load as W3 but only 3 sets, ensuring CNS recovery while keeping the pattern trained. 
      • WARMUP Hip mobility + banded walks 10min
      • WORK Deload — same load, 3 sets, crisp reps only Conventional Deadlift 3×4 RPE6
      • WORK Deload — reduced volume Bulgarian Split Squat 2×8 RPE6
      • WORK Deload — glute activation focus Hip Thrust (Barbell) 2×10 RPE6
      • COOLDOWN Hip flexor + hamstring stretch 5min
  Fri
    AM: [strength] Deload Upper B — Vertical Push/Pull — Final session of the deload — low volume, preserved intensity. Exit this session feeling fresh and ready to attack the next block. 
      • WARMUP Shoulder warm-up + face pulls 10min
      • WORK Deload — same load, 3 sets only Overhead Press (Barbell) 3×5 RPE6
      • WORK Deload — reduced volume Pull-Up / Lat Pulldown 3×6 RPE6
      • WORK Deload accessory Single-Arm Dumbbell Row 2×10 RPE6
      • WORK Shoulder health — light Face Pull (Cable) 2×15 RPE5
      • WORK Deload accessory Hammer Curl 2×10 RPE6
      • COOLDOWN Shoulder + lat stretch 5min
  Sat (REST)
    (full rest)
  Sun (REST)
    (full rest)
